title: Docker prints "unsupported manifest media type" for the image on Quay.io created by Podman.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

This issue is what I reported at https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2317#issuecomment-1263416447 originally.

Docker (`moby-engine-20.10.17-5.fc36.x86_64`) fails to read the manifest printing the message below.

```
$ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility:latest
unsupported manifest media type and no default available: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json

$ echo $?
1
```

The `skopeo` prints the manifest is version 2.1.

```
$ rpm -qf /bin/skopeo
skopeo-1.9.2-1.fc36.x86_64

$ skopeo inspect --raw docker://quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
{"schemaVersion":2,"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json","config":{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json","digest":"sha256:57c11c1b0746be91acbefc19c4086065eeed25cdece9b4a735a7bf7db1a6704f","size":842},"layers":[{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip","digest":"sha256:4d5653b00f8e5c9fe58400d000a745edd111bf51ddeddc595048ebebf10d3233","size":804285},{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip","digest":"sha256:fd38a27635a1dd7d1dcee9c442d95a0ea722fd2527ceef36ce7f62061d4ec830","size":339}],"annotations":{"org.opencontainers.image.base.digest":"sha256:505e5e20edbb5f2ac0abe3622358daf2f4a4c818eea0498445b7248e39db6728","org.opencontainers.image.base.name":"docker.io/library/busybox:1.35.0"}}
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

The reproducer with the `Dockerfile` is [here](https://github.com/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility). The used container image is [here](https://quay.io/repository/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox) .

1. `$ podman build --rm -t quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility:latest .`
2. `$ podman login quay.io`
3. `$ podman push quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility:latest`
4. `$ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
unsupported manifest media type and no default available: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json

$ echo $?
1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The `docker manifest inspect` prints the manifest data.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 16:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.0-2.fc36.x86_64
```

**Other info**

My environment is Fedora 36.

I used the docker below. It is the `moby-engine-20.10.17-5.fc36.x86_64` RPM package.

```
$ rpm -qf /bin/docker
moby-engine-20.10.17-5.fc36.x86_64
```

```
$ docker version
Client:

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No. What I tested is the Podman version 4.2.0. The latest version on the Fedora 36 is `4:4.2.1-2.fc36`. I will test with the latest version later.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

* Host OS is Fedora Linux 36.

comments.body:
> What I tested is the Podman version 4.2.0. The latest version on the Fedora 36 is 4:4.2.1-2.fc36. I will test with the latest version later.

I tested with the Podman latest version `podman-4.2.1-2.fc36.x86_64` now. It seems that the result is same.

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.1-2.fc36.x86_64

$ podman build --rm -t quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox:latest .

$ podman push quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
```

Here is the registory page.
https://quay.io/repository/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox

```
$ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox:latest

$ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
unsupported manifest media type and no default available: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json

$ echo $?
1

$ skopeo inspect --raw docker://quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-4.2.1-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
{"schemaVersion":2,"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json","config":{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json","digest":"sha256:853df3ca308c0b50a36be84c344edeed0fa59adc2e27145903ea661c6dfb7e4f","size":841},"layers":[{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip","digest":"sha256:4d5653b00f8e5c9fe58400d000a745edd111bf51ddeddc595048ebebf10d3233","size":804285},{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip","digest":"sha256:3ad0a7f89919c8a6c377274a114d4f6b4dd31ed136e21592c87f7eb44915cadb","size":348}],"annotations":{"org.opencontainers.image.base.digest":"sha256:505e5e20edbb5f2ac0abe3622358daf2f4a4c818eea0498445b7248e39db6728","org.opencontainers.image.base.name":"docker.io/library/busybox:1.35.0"}}
```

> ```
> $ docker manifest inspect quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility:latest
> unsupported manifest media type and no default available: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json
> ```

So this claims that the image is a Docker schema1 image. Docker has, IIRC, _completely removed_ support for that (old) format.

> ```
> $ skopeo inspect --raw docker://quay.io/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox:latest
> {"schemaVersion":2,"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",…
> ```

But this is a OCIv1 image, not a schema1 image. And given how `--raw` works, I rather trust this data. Unless, I guess, the registry was returning different manifests based on request headers.


> The used container image is [here](https://quay.io/repository/junaruga/report-podman-image-compatibility-busybox) .

This is not accessible to me.

---

From a quick look, https://github.com/docker/cli/blob/1d6c6e2367e292950e483e3c8ace142792bca718/cli/registry/client/fetcher.go#L30-L41 just doesn’t support OCI formats at all. So I _guess_ that the schema1 reference is an error handling or auto detection bug as a secondary consequence of the primary lack of support, but I haven’t gone looking for it.

So, assuming that hypothesis:
- If `docker manifest` doesn’t work with OCI, that needs to be fixed in Docker. (https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/43126 ? But that fails differently.)
- IIRC `podman build` does have an option to use the Docker schema2 format instead of OCI. I’m not immediately sure what is the current default nor I have a strong opinion on what _should_ be the default.

> This is not accessible to me.

Oh, sorry. Now I made it accessible (public).

It's probably due the fact that reference points to a single image and not a manifest list or image index.  Dockers error message could be better though.

I am closing as I do not see it being related to Podman.
Sure, that’s not an OCI image.
